title: Christ our Intercessor
summary: “My little children, these things write I unto you, that ye sin not. And if any man sin, we have an advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous.” (1John 2:1). What is the role of an advocate? The Greek word, paraklētos , means “one who pleads another's cause before a judge, a pleader, counsel for defense, legal assistant, an advocate”, according to James Strong. One needs an advocate because an advocate is trained in the language and custom of the law. We have all been summoned before the heavenly court of Christ’s judgement seat. “For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ; that every one may receive the things done in his body, according to that he hath done, whether it be good or bad” (2 Cor 5:10). The Lord Jesus offers himself to us as our advocate. It would be downright foolish to reject this gracious offer. In his novel, The Trial, Franz Kafka (1883- 1924), wrote about a man who was accused of crimes he had no knowledge of and was summoned to a court were the charges were not read out to him. Be assured that everyone will have perfect knowledge of their sins when they appear before the righteous Judge of all the earth.
